subject: History
source: Liberia Ministry of Education National Curriculum

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the nature and purpose, disprove the myth that Africa has no history and identify the sources of African history. Learners are able to discuss the Role of North Africa, the inhabitants, the invasion and place of Egypt, and the events of Meroe and Napata in the invasion of North Africa.
objectives: Describe the nature and purpose of African History
Explain the myth about African history
Identify the sources of African History
Discuss the role of North Africa (inhabitants, the invasion, the place of Egypt) in African History
Describe the Origin of KUSH (CUSH, CASH) methodology, history, conquest of Egypt by Kush and its impact
Discuss the main events of Meroe and Napata Kushtic history
contents: Nature and purpose of African history
Myth of African History
Sources of African History
Role of North Africa: inhabitants, the invasion, the place of Egypt
The Origin of Kush: methodology, history, conquest of Egypt
Events of Meroe and Napata Kushtic history
activities: Learners describe nature and purpose of African history
Research and disprove the myth that Africa has no history
Research and identify the sources of African History with the aid of maps, textbooks and the Internet
Individually learners discuss the Role of North Africa with focus on its inhabitants, European invasion and place of Egypt
Discuss the beginning of KUSH, the conquest of KUSH, conquest of Egypt by KUSH, decline and fall of KUSH
Discuss Meroe and Napata Kushtic History
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss Ethiopian History and explain the roles of King Solomon and Queen Sheba; Swahili Civilization, culture and history; and identify the origin of African Societies and civilization of ancient Sudan.
objectives: Explain the place of King Solomon and the Queen of Sheba in Ethiopian History
Discuss the special contribution of Ethiopia to African History
Identify those who contributed to the development of Swahili Civilization and Culture (Africans, Asians, Chinese and Arabs)
Discuss the literary contributions of the Swahili Civilization to African history
Discuss the Origin of African Societies and the Civilization of Ancient Sudan
contents: Ethiopian History: King Solomon, Queen Sheba
Contributions of Ethiopia to African history
Swahili Civilization and Culture
Ancient Sudan Civilization
Origin of African Societies and Civilization of Ancient Sudan
activities: Learners identify the contributions made by King Solomon and Queen Sheba to Ethiopian History
Individually research Ethiopian contributions to African History for class discussions
Pair learners to discuss Swahili Civilization, Culture and literary contributions to African history
Group learners to discuss the Origin of African Societies and Civilization of Ancient Sudan
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.topic: Liberian History – Foundation, Economy, and the 1980 Coup
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the foundation of the Liberian state, territorial expansion and encroachment and social conditions in Liberia. Learners are able to expound on the economic system of Liberia, identify some contributing industries and discuss some consequences that led to the 1980 Coup in Liberia.
objectives: Describe the New State and its Government
Analyze the Territorial Expansion and Encroachment on Liberia's land
Analyze and describe Social Conditions in Liberia
Discuss the Liberian Economy
Analyze the presidential terms and contributions of Four Selected Presidents
Discuss Foreign Relations
Analyze the Coup of 1980
contents: Foundation of the New State
Declaration of Independence: kind of state, system of government, powers of the president
Three branches of the Government
The driving force of Liberia's expansion
European encroachment and Government's stance
Social Condition: Role of Ministry of Education, tradition and cultural ties, Christian missionary contribution to education, Islam in Liberian Society
The economy system of Liberia: extractive industry, agriculture industry, foreign concessions liberalization policy
The 1980 Coup: root causes, shortcomings of the coup makers, Organization of the People's Redemption Council (PRC)
activities: Group learners to analyze the foundation of the New State and list some challenges faced
Group learners to discuss the Declaration of Independence as it relates to kind of government, system of government and powers of the president
Pair learners to distinguish the three branches of government and state the functions of each
Individual seat work to critique the driving force of Liberia's expansion
Classwork to analyze the European encroachment on Liberia's territory and state its consequences
Mixed Group to describe the Social Condition of Liberia, research the economic system, and analyze the 1980 Coup
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the Ghana Empire, the roles of its founders, contributions of Islamic Berbers, the organization and administration of the empire. Learners are able to analyze the trade and commerce and describe the rise and fall of the Ghana Empire and appraise the role of Ghana in African History.
objectives: Discuss the geographical and commercial factors surrounding the rise of the Ghana Empire
Explain the factors responsible for the disintegration of Ghana Empire
Discuss the relationship between old Ghana Empire and the former Gold Coast
contents: Black African founders of Ghana
Contributions of Islamized Berbers
Growth and expansion of Ghana
Organization/Administration
Trade and Commerce
Decline and fall of Ghana Empire
Ghana in African History (colonialism and independence)
activities: Individual learners discuss the role and activities of black founders of Ghana and the contributions of Islamized Berbers
Group learners to analyze the expansion and growth of the Ghana Empire
Pair learners to describe the trade and commerce of the Ghana Empire
Group learners to analyze the organization and administration, describe conditions that led to the rise and fall, and discuss the role of Ghana in African History
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the foundation of the Mali Empire, its founders; the roles of Kangaba and Kaniaga in the emergence of Mali and the glory of Mansa Musa and Sundiata Keita, as well as analyzing the importance of Mali in African history.
objectives: Describe the roles which Kangaba and Kaniaga played in the emergence of Mali
Describe the glory of Mansa Musa in Mali Empire
contents: Sundiata Keita and the founding of the Mali Empire
Expansion and administration of Mansa Musa
The circumstances leading to the fall of Mali
The importance of Mali in African History
activities: Describe the origin of the Mali Empire
Role play Mansa Musa at the height of his power
Collect information on Sundiata Keita, Mansa Musa and other kings of Mali
Discuss the commerce and trade of Mali
Analyze the role of Mali in African History
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the origin and greatness of the Songhai Empire, factors that led to its decline and existing relationship between Africa and the world.
objectives: Discuss the origin of the Songhai Empire
Describe the developments leading to the greatness of the Songhai Empire
Analyze the factors that led to the decline of Songhai
Discuss the relations which existed between Africa and the world
contents: Early occupation of the Songhai People
Government of Songhai
Annexation of Gao by Songhai
Cessation of the Gao from Songhai, expansion and administration
Rise and fall of Songhai
Relations which existed between Africa and the world
The importance of Songhai in African History
activities: Distinguish how each of the occupations was helpful in the expansion of the Empire
Research and analyze the government of Songhai
Analyze why Morocco invaded Songhai
Discuss the exploits of Sunni Ali II
Role-play Songhai Empire under Sunni Ali I and Sunni Ali II
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss and analyze the origins, growth, development and contributions of other empires in the history of Africa.
objectives: Discuss the founding, growth and development of Kanem Bornu State
Analyze the origin of the Hausa State and the role of Islam
Describe the origin and expansion of the Mossi and Akan States
Discuss the origin of the Yoruba state of Oyo and Benin
Analyze the contribution of iron technology to the expansion of Bakongo Empire
Discuss the origin, expansion and administration of the Monomotapa Empire
contents: Founding and Growth of Kanem Bornu
Origin of Hausa State
Origin and expansion of Mossi and Akan States
Cultural contributions of Yoruba in Oyo and Benin
Iron Technology and expansion of Bakongo Empire
The Monomotapa Empire
activities: Describe the founding, growth and administration of Kanem Bornu Empire
Analyze the origin and expansion of the Mossi and Akan State
Analyze and explain the contributions of Yoruba in Oyo and Benin States
Explain the role of Islam as a force in the Hausa State
Research and describe the foundation of Iron Technology and expansion of Bakongo Empire
Analyze the Cultural Differences in the Expansion of Monomotapa
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.unit: The Great Slave Trade, Colonization and Exploration
topics.topic: The Great Slave Trade, Colonization and Exploration
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss and describe the Great Slave Trade, Colonization and the Exploration of Africa by European Powers.
objectives: Discuss the genesis of the Slave Trade
Describe the exploration of Africa
Analyze the effects of African Colonization
Describe African History: Problems and Prospects
Analyze European History: Fertile Crescent
Discuss the impacts of the Egyptian Civilization
contents: The Great Slave Trade
Exploration of Africa by Europeans
Effects of Colonization
Problems and Prospects associated with the colonization
Fertile Crescent
Impacts of Egyptian Civilization
activities: Pair learners to discuss the origin and impacts of the Great Slave Trade
Discuss the major objectives of the exploration of Africa
Analyze the effects of Colonization in Africa
Mixed Groups to research and discuss the Problems and Prospects that impacted the Colonization
Describe the Fertile Crescent as a cradle of Western Civilization
Analyze the impact of the Egyptian Civilization and its contributions to Africa and the World
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners will be able to appreciate the contributions made by the forerunners of European civilization.
objectives: Discuss European history and its periods
Identify the three pillars of European history
Discuss the Fertile Crescent as the birth place of world religions and civilization
Discuss the contributions of the Tigris and Euphrates rivers to the civilizations of the Fertile Crescent
contents: European History: definition and purpose of history, prehistory, the three periods in history and their characteristics
The three pillars of Western civilization
The Fertile Crescent as the cradle of Western civilization
The civilizations of Sumer, Akkahad, Babylon, Assyria, Persia, and their unique contributions
activities: Individual work: Discuss the meaning and purpose of history, and European prehistory; name and discuss the Three Periods in European history
Group A discusses the Fertile Crescent as cradle of western civilization
Group B discusses the Sumer and Akkahad civilizations
Group C discusses the Babylonian and Assyrian civilizations
Group D discusses the Persians
Each group highlights the major contributions of their assigned subject to the growth of European civilization
materials: World History in the 20th Century - Longman

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate knowledge of European history from Ancient Greece to the Roman Empire.
objectives: Discuss the migration and settlement of the Greeks
Discuss the nature of the Greek mind and their contribution of philosophy to Western civilization
Analyze the rise and fall of Athens and Sparta
Explain the rise of Macedonia and its conquest of Greece
Recount the exploits of Alexander the Great
Describe the features of the Hellenistic age
Explain the mythological and historical origins of Rome
Discuss the growth and achievements of the Roman republic and empire
Discuss the Barbarian exploits and the impact of the Church on the conquerors
Analyze the contribution of Rome to Western civilization
contents: Migration and settlement of the Greeks
Greek Minds on Western Civilization and Philosophy: Pre-Socratic philosophy, Socrates and the Sophists, Plato and Aristotle
The rise and fall of Athens and Sparta
Macedonia: The rise of Macedonia and its conquest of Greece, The campaign of Alexander the Great
The Hellenistic age
Rome: The origins of Rome, Expansion and administration of the republic and the empire, The Barbarian invasion, The fall of the empire
Roman achievements and contribution to Western civilization
Christianity and the Empire
activities: Mixed Group work: Examine the nature of the Greek migration and the emergence of Greek city-states
Individual work: Trace the origins of democracy to Ancient Greek political institutions; explain the rivalry between Athens and Sparta
Group 1 discusses the Greeks' Pre-Socratic philosophy
Group 2 discusses Socrates and the Sophists
Group 3 discusses Plato and Aristotle
Home work: Investigate the Trojan Wars and present before the class
Individual work: Locate Macedonia on a map; discuss the rise of Macedonia and its conquest of Greece
Mixed Group work: Discuss major events of the Hellenistic Age; examine mythological and historical events associated with the rise of the Roman empire
Individual work: Discuss the role of Christianity in the Roman Empire; discuss the Barbarian invasion; present analysis of Rome's contribution to Western civilization
materials: World History in the 20th Century - Longman

topics.topic: The Advent of Christianity and the Reformations
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to accept and relate to the teachings of Christianity.
objectives: Discuss Christianity in terms of its Judaic origins, its founder and teachings
Discuss the spread of Christianity, the rise of Papal infallibility and the modification of Papal infallibility
Analyze the Protestant and Catholic Reformations
Explain the rise of nationalism in Europe
contents: Christianity: The Judaic origin of Christianity, Jesus Christ the man, Christ's basic teachings, The rise and the decline of the Papacy
Developments leading to the Protestant Reformation: Martin Luther, John Calvin, St. Ignatius Loyola
Results of the protest
Root causes of the Catholic Reformation
Impact of the Catholic Reformation
The rise of nationalism in Europe
Major European nationalists
activities: Individual work: Cite Scriptural references in the Bible that tell of the Birth of Christ, death and Resurrection; present to the class
Mixed Group work: Discuss the basic beliefs of Christianity; cite why Catholics consider Saint Peter the first Pope
Debate what crucial factors contributed to Christ's teachings surviving the persecution in the Church's infancy
Debate the rise and decline of the Papacy
Individual work: Explain the factors that led to the Protestant Reformation; compare and contrast the role of Martin Luther, John Calvin and St. Ignatius Loyola
Home work: Research the key elements reached at the Council of Trent and present to the class
Individual work: Discuss how common national identity was a crucial factor in the rise of nationalism in Europe; brainstorm the connection between nationalism and the start of World War I
materials: World History in the 20th Century - Longman

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to analyze events that characterized the Middle Ages from 500AD to 1000AD.
objectives: Identify the three periods in the Middle Ages
State the social, economic and political characteristics of each period
Describe the barbarian invasion and its impact on Europe
Explain the relationship between the church and the invaders
Discuss university scholasticism
Analyze the growth of towns and expansion of trade in Europe
contents: The Middle Ages: Medieval society
The rise of Feudalism: Manorialists
The Middle Ages as the Dark Ages
Period of the Barbarian invasions: formation of the Germanic nation and the consolidation of the German holy nation
Socialization of the Germans by the Church
Establishment of the Holy Roman Empire
The rise of universities
Towns and expansion of trade
Scholasticism
Period of great Church influence
activities: Individual work: Discuss some of the main conditions by which the Middle Ages is characterized
Group 1 discusses the political characteristics of Medieval society
Group 2 discusses the role and living condition of the serfs and the vassals
Group 3 discusses the structure of the manorial system and its consequences on society
Group D gives a general overview of the Feudal system vis-à-vis the kings and lords
Mixed Group work: Discuss why the Middle Ages was referred to as the Dark Ages

Individual work: Research the establishment of the Holy Roman Empire; explain the relationship between the church and the invaders; discuss the Germans' conquest of the Romans; research the establishment of the universities of Paris and Oxford
Mixed Group work: Discuss the contribution of St. Thomas Aquinas to Scholasticism
materials: World History in the 20th Century - Longman

topics.unit: The Industrial Revolution and the Enlightenment
topics.topic: The Industrial Revolution and the Enlightenment
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to analyze the significance of the Industrial Revolution in Europe and America, and how it resulted in the abolition of the slave trade; develop an appreciation for the contributions made by the people of the Enlightenment period.
objectives: Discuss the social and economic conditions which led to the Industrial Revolution
Analyze the impact of the Industrial Revolution on Europe and America
Discuss the Enlightenment in terms of its primary aims and objectives
contents: The Industrial Revolution: contributing factors, centers in Europe, impact on European industry and plantations in America, impact on the great slave trade
The Enlightenment: the Enlightenment as an intellectual movement
English representatives of the Enlightenment: John Locke and Thomas Hobbes
French representatives of the Enlightenment: Rousseau, Voltaire and Montesquieu
activities: Mixed Group work: Discuss where the Industrial Revolution began and factors that contributed to its spread throughout Europe
Pair work: How did the Industrial Revolution impact agrarian societies in Europe; how did it maximize production
Individual work: On a poster sheet, sketch some simple machines built during the Industrial Revolution; show how the machine would have worked
Mixed Group work: Discuss how the Industrial Revolution resulted in the abolition of slavery in America
Individual work: Discuss the characteristics of the Enlightenment and why that period was considered as the time of the Enlightenment; discuss the meaning and purpose of an intellectual movement
Home work: Compare and contrast the contributions of John Locke, J. J. Rousseau, Montesquieu, Voltaire to the Enlightenment
materials: World History in the 20th Century - Longman

topics.unit: Western Imperialism, the Age of Revolution and the World Wars
topics.topic: Western Imperialism, the Age of Revolution and the World Wars
topics.outcomes: Learners will be able to analyze periods in European history dating from the days of Western imperialism to the Second World War.
objectives: Discuss Imperialism as an age-old phenomenon
Explain the root causes of Western Imperialism
Analyze the impact of Colonialism on the African Continent
Discuss analytically the causes and impact of the French Revolution
Explain the factors that led to the two World Wars
contents: Western Imperialism and Colonialism: social and political roots, major Western imperial powers, colonialism as an offshoot of imperialism, social/political/economic impact
The colonial powers, their colonies and possessions
The internal and external factors of decolonization
The social and economic impact of colonization
The Age of Revolution: social, political and economic roots of the French Revolution; impact on French society
The social, political and economic roots of the American Revolution; impact on American society
World War I and the formation of the League of Nations
World War II and the formation of the United Nations
Liberia as founding member of the League of Nations and the United Nations
activities: Individual work: Discuss who were the major Western imperial powers and explain their methods of imperial domination
Group A researches the roots of Western imperialism
Group B researches the impact of colonialism on cultural degradation in Africa
Group C researches the colonial political structures in colonized African territories
Group D researches the social and economic benefits of decolonization on African societies
Individual work: Discuss analytically the causes and impact of the French Revolution; identify five leading figures of the French Revolution; explain root causes and impact of the American Revolution
Mixed Group work: Research to identify the impacts of the French and American revolutions on modern democracy

Pair work: Discuss factors that led to World War I and formation of the League of Nations; discuss factors that led to World War II and formation of the United Nations; discuss Liberia's role in the formation of both organizations
materials: World History in the 20th Century - Longman
Africa South of the Sahara - Longman

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss factors leading to Liberian Civil War, its causes, major players, role of ECOWAS.
objectives: Discuss the First Liberian Civil War
Identify the Essential Players in the Liberian Civil War and the Role of ECOWAS
Discuss the Interim Government of National Unity
Analyze the performances of the Liberian Transitional Governments up to 1997
Discuss the Elections held from 1997 to 2017
Analyze the positive and negative effects of the Civil War on the elderly, young adults, and children
Discuss the relationship between family size and poverty
contents: Liberian Civil War: causes of the war, major players, why the war lasted so long and level of destruction
The Role of ECOWAS
Interim Government of National Unity: Liberian Transitional Governments, The Kpormakpor Council, The Sankawulo Council, The Ruth Perry Council
activities: Discuss the causes of the Liberian Civil War, list major players and analyze why the war lasted so long
Describe the role of ECOWAS States and the gains made in achieving peace
Analyze the involvement of the Interim Governments/Administrations
Discuss the post-war elections from 2005 to 2017
Analyze the positive and negative effects of the Civil War on the elderly, young adults, and children
Discuss the relationship between family size and poverty
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the Foreign Policy of Liberia, role played in pursuing international relations with other nations and governments.
objectives: Discuss Liberia's vital and non-vital interests throughout the years up to 1920
Discuss the policy adopted to pursue these interests
Describe the role of Liberia in the OAU/AU, ECOWAS and the Mano River Union
contents: Foreign Policy Objectives
Formulation and implementation of foreign policy
Relations with Europe
Relations with the United States of America
The League of Nations/United Nations
Relations with Asia
Relations with OAU/AU, ECOWAS and Mano River Union
activities: Research and discuss the foreign policy objectives of Liberia
Research and describe how the foreign policies are formulated and subsequently implemented
Research and discuss the relation with USA
Research and discuss the founding of the League of Nations and United Nations
Research and describe the relation with Asia
Research and analyze Liberia's relation and interest in the OAU/AU, ECOWAS, and the Mano River Union
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson
The Foreign Policy of President William V.S. Tubman by Dr. D. Elwood Dunn
Annual Reports - Ministry of Foreign Affairs

topics.unit: Administrations of Seven Selected Liberian Presidents
topics.topic: The Administrations of Seven Selected Liberian Presidents (1847–1990)
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss and analyze the administrations, policies, and contributions towards the socioeconomic development of Liberia under the selected presidents.
objectives: Discuss the administration (term of office) of each of the selected presidents
Describe the policies of each toward national cohesion and growth
Discuss the contributions made by each of these presidents to the socio-economic development of Liberia
contents: Terms of office of the selected presidents
Policies of each toward national cohesion and growth
The unique contributions of the following Presidents: Joseph Jenkins Roberts, Daniel E. Howard, Arthur Barclay, Charles D.B. King, William V.S. Tubman, William R. Tolbert Jr., Samuel Kanyon Doe
activities: Mixed Groups: Describe the administrations/achievements of each president and submit a report
Discuss the Terms of Office of each president
Pair works: Discuss the policy of each president towards national cohesion and growth
Describe the major contribution made by each president
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the growth pattern of the Liberian Economy, the contributions of concessionaires/companies; including Agriculture and Forestry. Learners are also able to describe some factors that impede Liberian Economic Growth.
objectives: Describe the economic growth of Liberia from 1950 to present
Discuss the contributions of major concessionaires/companies
Describe the contributions of Agriculture and Forestry to economic growth of Liberia
Analyze the factors that have impeded the growth of the Liberian Economy
contents: Liberia's economic growth from 1950 to present
Contributions of Major Concessionaires: LAMCO, BONG MINES, NIOC, FIRESTONE RUBBER PLANTATION, LAC (Liberia Agricultural Company)
Agriculture and Forestry contributions to the economy
Factors impeding the growth of the economy
activities: Mixed Groups: Describe the economic growth of Liberia; differentiate the contributions of each concessionaire to the economy
Individual works: Discuss the roles of Agriculture and Forestry in Liberia
Discuss and analyze the factors that impede the growth and development of the Liberian Economy on education, health, family, and youths
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the role of Africa during the founding of the United Nations and Africa's contributions to the UN. Learners are also able to evaluate the relationship between African countries and the United Nations.
objectives: Discuss Africa and the United Nations
Identify countries in Africa that played key roles during the founding of the United Nations
Discuss Africa's contributions to the United Nations
Summarize the relationship between African countries and the United Nations
contents: Africa and United Nations
Africa's role at the founding of the United Nations
Contributions of Africa to the United Nations
Relationship between African Countries and the United Nations
activities: Individual works: Discuss Africa and United Nations; differentiate Africa's role during the founding of the United Nations from others
Mixed Groups: Discuss the contributions of Africa to the United Nations
Describe the relationship between African Countries and the United Nations
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson

topics.unit: The Struggle for Political Sovereignty in Eastern Africa
topics.topic: The Struggle for Political Sovereignty in Eastern Africa, 1945 to Independence
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the struggle for political sovereignty in Eastern Africa during the 20th century. Learners will become aware that some African women were victims of sexual exploitation and abuse during the political struggles.
objectives: Discuss the political struggle in Eastern Africa during the 20th century
Analyze the political struggle faced by African Nations toward independence
Discuss values and attitudes on sexual behavior
contents: The partitioning of Eastern Africa and the scramble of political powers
The political struggle in Eastern Africa during the 20th century
The political struggle faced by African Nations toward their independence
Sexual behavior
activities: Individual works: Draw the map of East Africa and list the countries in that region
Pair works: Discuss the partitioning of Eastern Africa
Describe the scramble for political powers in Eastern Africa
Mixed Groups: Discuss the political struggle in Eastern Africa during the 20th Century
Analyze the values and attitudes on sexual behavior (love, friendship, sexual attraction)
materials: History in Diagram for West Africa - Pearson
